Estimating Annual Non-Hispanic Black Population Share in Cook County, IL

A TWDeConv Example: Deconvolving ACS 5-Year Estimates and Validating Against the 2020 Decennial Census

TWDeConv Example

2026-03-04

1 Background

The ACS 5-year estimate for the proportion of non-Hispanic (NH) Black residents in Census tract \(i\) ending in year \(s\) is approximately:

\[P_{i,s} = \frac{1}{5}\sum_{t=s-4}^{s} X_{i,t} + \varepsilon_{i,s}\]

where \(X_{i,t}\) is the true annual NH Black population share — never directly observed at the annual, tract level. TWDeConv recovers \(X\) by solving a Kronecker-structured spatio-temporal generalised lasso via ADMM.

Because the observable \(P_{i,s}\) is a derived proportion (not a single ACS variable), we compute it and its standard error from two ACS variables:

Variable Description
B03002_001 Total population
B03002_004 Not Hispanic or Latino: Black or African American alone

\[P_{i,s} = \frac{\hat{X}^{\text{Black}}_{i,s}}{\hat{X}^{\text{Total}}_{i,s}}, \quad \widehat{\text{SE}}(P_{i,s}) = \frac{1}{\hat{X}^{\text{Total}}_{i,s}} \sqrt{\widehat{\text{SE}}^2_{\text{Black}} - P_{i,s}^2\, \widehat{\text{SE}}^2_{\text{Total}}}\]

(ACS ratio SE formula; falls back to the sum-of-squares form when the discriminant is negative.)

The recovered 2020 annual estimates are then validated against the 2020 Decennial Census PL 94-171 redistricting file — a full enumeration with no sampling error — making this the only example in the package with near-true ground truth at the tract level.

Cook County, IL contains ~1,300 Census tracts, a substantially larger problem than the St. Louis City income example.

2 Setup

library(TWDeConv)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(Matrix)

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

Census API key required. Register at https://api.census.gov/data/key_signup.html and install once with:

tidycensus::census_api_key("YOUR_KEY_HERE", install = TRUE)

3 Fetch and Prepare ACS Data

We use five consecutive ACS 5-year windows (end years 2018–2022):

  • \(S = 5\) observed windows
  • \(T = S + 4 = 9\) latent annual time points (2014–2022)

3.1 Step 1: Structural matrices from NH Black counts

prep_acs_for_deconv() is called with B03002_004 (NH Black count) to obtain the five structural matrices (\(Y\), \(M\), \(W\), \(D\), \(L_s\)) and the aligned GEOID set. We will replace \(Y\) and \(W\) with proportion-based counterparts in Step 2.

year_ends <- 2018:2022
S         <- length(year_ends)

inp_black <- prep_acs_for_deconv(
  state     = "IL",
  county    = "Cook",
  year_ends = year_ends,
  variables = c(black_nh = "B03002_004"),
  k         = 2L,
  queen     = TRUE,
  vre       = TRUE,
  cache_dir = "~/acs_cache"
)
## Error in load_tiger(url, tigris_type = "tract", ...) : 
##   unused argument (quiet = FALSE)

print(inp_black)
## ── DeconvInput (TWDeConv) ─────────────────────────────────
##   Geography : tract  |  Cook county, IL
##   Variable  : black_nh  (code: B03002_004)
##   Windows   : 5  (ACS end years 2018 – 2022)
##   Latent yrs: 9  (2014 – 2022)
##   Units (n) : 1288
##   Y dims    : 1288 × 5  (units × windows)
##   M dims    : 5 × 9  (windows × latent years)
##   W dims    : 6440 × 6440  (diagonal precision)
##   D dims    : 7 × 9  (order-2 temporal diff)
##   Ls dims   : 1288 × 1288  (spatial Laplacian)
##   SE source : moe
## 
##   Pass to solver:
##     solver <- ADMMSolver$new(
##       Y=inp$Y, M=inp$M, W=inp$W, D=inp$D, Ls=inp$Ls,
##       lambda_t=..., lambda_s=..., rho=1, ridge=1e-4
##     )
## ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
common_geoids <- rownames(inp_black$Y)
n_tracts      <- length(common_geoids)
cat("Common tracts across all windows:", n_tracts, "\n")
## Common tracts across all windows: 1288

3.2 Step 2: Fetch total population (denominator)

total_list <- vector("list", S)
for (i in seq_len(S)) {
  total_list[[i]] <- fetch_acs_vre_data(
    state     = "IL",
    county    = "Cook",
    year_end  = year_ends[i],
    variables = c(total = "B03002_001"),
    geometry  = FALSE,
    vre       = FALSE,          # VRE not needed for denominator
    cache_dir = "~/acs_cache",
    quiet     = TRUE
  )
}

3.3 Step 3: Compute proportion matrix Y and precision weights W

# -- NH Black counts and their SEs (n x S) ----------------------------------
Y_count  <- inp_black$Y
SE_count <- matrix(
  sqrt(1 / Matrix::diag(inp_black$W)),
  nrow = n_tracts, ncol = S,
  dimnames = list(common_geoids, colnames(Y_count))
)

# -- Total population and its SE (n x S), aligned to common_geoids ----------
denom_mat    <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n_tracts, ncol = S,
                       dimnames = list(common_geoids, colnames(Y_count)))
SE_denom_mat <- denom_mat

for (i in seq_len(S)) {
  g_i <- rownames(total_list[[i]]$estimates)
  idx <- match(common_geoids, g_i)
  denom_mat[, i]    <- total_list[[i]]$estimates[idx, "total"]
  SE_denom_mat[, i] <- total_list[[i]]$se[idx, "total"]
}

# -- Proportion: P = NH Black / Total ---------------------------------------
Y_prop <- Y_count / denom_mat

# -- SE of proportion (ACS ratio formula) ------------------------------------
SE_prop <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n_tracts, ncol = S,
                  dimnames = dimnames(Y_prop))

for (i in seq_len(S)) {
  N    <- denom_mat[, i]
  p    <- Y_prop[, i]
  se_x <- SE_count[, i]
  se_n <- SE_denom_mat[, i]

  disc        <- se_x^2 - p^2 * se_n^2
  SE_prop[, i] <- ifelse(
    !is.na(disc) & disc >= 0,
    sqrt(disc)                      / N,   # standard ACS ratio formula
    sqrt(se_x^2 + p^2 * se_n^2)    / N    # fallback (sum form)
  )
}

# Guard: set SE to NA where total is zero or missing
SE_prop[is.na(denom_mat) | denom_mat == 0] <- NA_real_

cat("Y_prop range  :", round(range(Y_prop, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")
## Y_prop range  : 0 1
cat("SE_prop range :", round(range(SE_prop, na.rm = TRUE), 5), "\n")
## SE_prop range : 9e-05 0.91416
# Precision weight matrix (NA SEs produce W=0, excluding from WLS)
W_prop <- build_precision_weights(SE_prop)
cat("W diagonal range:", round(range(Matrix::diag(W_prop)), 1), "\n")
## W diagonal range: 0 121715507

4 Solve the Deconvolution

Proportions live on \([0, 1]\), so \(\lambda\) values are much smaller than for dollar-denominated income. We use \(\lambda_t = 1\) and \(\lambda_s = 0.1\).

Note: With ~1,300 Cook County tracts the one-time Cholesky factorisation in $setup() may take 1–2 minutes.

X_hat <- solve_deconv(
  Y        = Y_prop,
  M        = inp_black$M,
  W        = W_prop,
  D        = inp_black$D,
  Ls       = inp_black$Ls,
  lambda_t = 1,
  lambda_s = 0.1,
  rho      = 1.0,
  max_iter = 5000,
  verbose  = TRUE
)
##   [ADMM    1]  r_pri=1.174e+01  r_dual=4.899e+02   eps_pri=7.816e-02  eps_dual=7.737e-02
##   [ADMM  100]  r_pri=2.980e-01  r_dual=2.187e+00   eps_pri=1.024e-01  eps_dual=1.262e-01
##   [ADMM  200]  r_pri=1.101e-01  r_dual=6.437e-01   eps_pri=1.024e-01  eps_dual=1.272e-01
##   [ADMM  300]  r_pri=5.858e-02  r_dual=3.012e-01   eps_pri=1.023e-01  eps_dual=1.276e-01
##   [ADMM  400]  r_pri=3.581e-02  r_dual=1.582e-01   eps_pri=1.023e-01  eps_dual=1.278e-01
##   Converged at iteration 439.
# Clip to [0, 1]: proportions must be bounded
X_hat <- pmax(pmin(X_hat, 1), 0)

cat("Recovered dimensions:", nrow(X_hat), "tracts x", ncol(X_hat), "years\n")
## Recovered dimensions: 1288 tracts x 9 years
cat("X_hat range:", round(range(X_hat, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")
## X_hat range: 0 1
cat("Latent years:",
    inp_black$metadata$first_latent_year, "–",
    inp_black$metadata$last_latent_year, "\n")
## Latent years: 2014 – 2022

4.1 Convergence diagnostic

solver <- solve_deconv(
  Y             = Y_prop,
  M             = inp_black$M,
  W             = W_prop,
  D             = inp_black$D,
  Ls            = inp_black$Ls,
  lambda_t      = 1,
  lambda_s      = 0.1,
  rho           = 1.0,
  max_iter      = 5000,
  return_solver = TRUE
)
solver$print()
## ── ADMMSolver (TWDeConv) ──────────────────────────────────
##   Problem  : 1288 cells × 9 latent years | 5 observed periods
##   Params   : λ_t=1  λ_s=0.1  ρ=1  ridge=1e-08
##   Setup    : done
##   Iters    : 439  |  Converged: TRUE
##   Final    : r_primal=3.059e-02  r_dual=1.270e-01
##   Objective: total=751.9  (data=121.7  temp=103.9  spat=526.4)
## ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(solver$history, aes(x = iter)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = r_primal, colour = "Primal residual")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = r_dual,   colour = "Dual residual")) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Primal residual" = "#2c7bb6", "Dual residual" = "#d7191c"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  labs(title = "ADMM convergence",
       x = "Iteration", y = "Residual norm (log scale)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

5 Results

5.1 Long-format data frame

latent_years <- inp_black$metadata$first_latent_year:inp_black$metadata$last_latent_year

X_long <- as.data.frame(X_hat)
colnames(X_long) <- as.character(latent_years)
X_long$GEOID     <- rownames(X_hat)

X_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(
  X_long,
  cols      = -GEOID,
  names_to  = "year",
  values_to = "prop_black_hat"
)
X_long$year <- as.integer(X_long$year)

5.2 Time series: selected tracts

# Observed proportions in long format
Y_long <- as.data.frame(Y_prop)
colnames(Y_long) <- as.character(year_ends)
Y_long$GEOID     <- rownames(Y_prop)

Y_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(
  Y_long,
  cols      = -GEOID,
  names_to  = "year_end",
  values_to = "prop_obs"
)
Y_long$year_end <- as.integer(Y_long$year_end)

# Select 5 representative tracts spanning the NH Black share distribution
mean_prop  <- rowMeans(Y_prop, na.rm = TRUE)
sel_geoids <- names(sort(mean_prop))[
  round(seq(1, sum(!is.na(mean_prop)), length.out = 5))
]

X_sel <- X_long[X_long$GEOID %in% sel_geoids, ]
Y_sel <- Y_long[Y_long$GEOID %in% sel_geoids, ]

ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = Y_sel,
    aes(x = year_end - 4, xend = year_end,
        y = prop_obs, yend = prop_obs, colour = GEOID),
    linewidth = 2.5, alpha = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = X_sel,
    aes(x = year, y = prop_black_hat, colour = GEOID),
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = X_sel,
    aes(x = year, y = prop_black_hat, colour = GEOID),
    size = 2
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = latent_years) +
  labs(
    title    = "Recovered annual vs. observed 5-year NH Black population share",
    subtitle = "Thick segments = ACS 5-year observed windows; lines = recovered annual signal",
    x = "Year", y = "NH Black share",
    colour = "Census tract (GEOID)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.text     = element_text(size = 7))

5.3 Spatial maps: recovered share by year

tracts_sf <- tidycensus::get_acs(
  geography = "tract",
  variables = "B03002_001",
  state     = "IL",
  county    = "Cook",
  year      = 2022,
  survey    = "acs5",
  geometry  = TRUE
) |>
  dplyr::select(GEOID, geometry) |>
  dplyr::distinct(GEOID, .keep_all = TRUE)

|=================================================================     |  93%  |                                                                              |==================================================================    |  94%  |                                                                              |===================================================================   |  95%  |                                                                              |===================================================================   |  96%  |                                                                              |====================================================================  |  97%  |                                                                              |===================================================================== |  99%  |                                                                              |======================================================================| 100%
years_to_map <- c(2014, 2016, 2018, 2020, 2022)

map_df <- X_long |>
  dplyr::filter(year %in% years_to_map) |>
  dplyr::left_join(tracts_sf, by = "GEOID") |>
  sf::st_as_sf()

ggplot(map_df) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = prop_black_hat), colour = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~ year, ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    option   = "magma", direction = -1,
    name     = "NH Black\nshare",
    labels   = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    na.value = "grey80"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Recovered annual NH Black population share — Cook County tracts",
    subtitle = paste0("TWDeConv  |  \u03bb_t = 1,  \u03bb_s = 0.1  |  Latent years ",
                      min(latent_years), "\u2013", max(latent_years))
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 9)
  )

6 Validation: Comparison with 2020 Decennial Census

6.1 Why the Decennial Census works here

Unlike income (which the Decennial does not collect), racial composition is measured by the Decennial Census as a full enumeration with no sampling error. The 2020 PL 94-171 redistricting file provides:

Variable Description
P2_001N Total population
P2_006N Not Hispanic or Latino: Black or African American alone

Two caveats apply:

  1. Timing: the Decennial is a single-day snapshot (April 1, 2020); the deconvolved estimate represents an annual average. In rapidly changing tracts these may differ.
  2. Mode effects: mail/online survey (ACS) vs. enumerator (Decennial) can produce small differences in self-reported race.

6.2 Fetch Decennial 2020 tract data

dec_raw <- tidycensus::get_decennial(
  geography = "tract",
  variables = c(total    = "P2_001N",
                black_nh = "P2_006N"),
  state     = "IL",
  county    = "Cook",
  year      = 2020,
  sumfile   = "pl"
)

dec_2020 <- dec_raw |>
  dplyr::select(GEOID, variable, value) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = variable, values_from = value) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    prop_black_dec = dplyr::if_else(total > 0, black_nh / total, NA_real_)
  )

cat("Decennial tracts:", nrow(dec_2020), "\n")
## Decennial tracts: 1332
cat("Proportion range:", round(range(dec_2020$prop_black_dec, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")
## Proportion range: 0.001 0.974

6.3 Agreement statistics

comp_df <- X_long |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2020) |>
  dplyr::inner_join(dec_2020, by = "GEOID") |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(prop_black_hat), !is.na(prop_black_dec))

rmse <- sqrt(mean((comp_df$prop_black_hat - comp_df$prop_black_dec)^2))
mae  <- mean(abs(comp_df$prop_black_hat  - comp_df$prop_black_dec))
bias <- mean(comp_df$prop_black_hat      - comp_df$prop_black_dec)
r    <- cor(comp_df$prop_black_hat, comp_df$prop_black_dec)

cat("=== TWDeConv 2020 vs. Decennial 2020 (Cook County tracts) ===\n")
## === TWDeConv 2020 vs. Decennial 2020 (Cook County tracts) ===
cat(sprintf("  Tracts compared : %d\n",      nrow(comp_df)))
##   Tracts compared : 1286
cat(sprintf("  Pearson r       : %.4f\n",    r))
##   Pearson r       : 0.9845
cat(sprintf("  RMSE            : %.4f  (%.1f pct pts)\n", rmse, rmse * 100))
##   RMSE            : 0.0615  (6.2 pct pts)
cat(sprintf("  MAE             : %.4f  (%.1f pct pts)\n", mae,  mae  * 100))
##   MAE             : 0.0373  (3.7 pct pts)
cat(sprintf("  Mean bias       : %+.4f (%+.1f pct pts)\n", bias, bias * 100))
##   Mean bias       : -0.0044 (-0.4 pct pts)

6.4 Scatter plot: TWDeConv vs. Decennial

ggplot(comp_df, aes(x = prop_black_dec, y = prop_black_hat)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35, size = 1.2, colour = "#2c7bb6") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0,
              colour = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", colour = "#d7191c",
              se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.9) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 1)) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.05, y = 0.92, hjust = 0, size = 3.8,
           label = sprintf("r = %.3f\nRMSE = %.1f pct pts\nBias = %+.1f pct pts",
                           r, rmse * 100, bias * 100)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Deconvolved 2020 NH Black share vs. 2020 Decennial Census",
    subtitle = "Dashed = perfect agreement  |  Red = OLS fit  |  Each point = one Census tract",
    x        = "Decennial Census 2020 (full count)",
    y        = "TWDeConv deconvolved 2020"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

6.5 Side-by-side maps: TWDeConv 2020 vs. Decennial 2020

map_comp <- comp_df |>
  dplyr::select(GEOID, prop_black_hat, prop_black_dec) |>
  dplyr::left_join(tracts_sf, by = "GEOID") |>
  sf::st_as_sf() |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(prop_black_hat, prop_black_dec),
    names_to  = "source",
    values_to = "prop_black"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(source = dplyr::recode(source,
    prop_black_hat = "TWDeConv 2020",
    prop_black_dec = "Decennial Census 2020"
  ))

ggplot(map_comp) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = prop_black), colour = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~ source) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    option   = "magma", direction = -1,
    name     = "NH Black\nshare",
    labels   = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    na.value = "grey80"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "NH Black population share: TWDeConv 2020 vs. Decennial Census 2020",
    subtitle = "Cook County Census tracts"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

6.6 Residual map: TWDeConv − Decennial

map_resid <- comp_df |>
  dplyr::mutate(resid = prop_black_hat - prop_black_dec) |>
  dplyr::left_join(tracts_sf, by = "GEOID") |>
  sf::st_as_sf()

lim <- max(abs(map_resid$resid), na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(map_resid) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = resid), colour = NA) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette  = "RdBu",
    limits   = c(-lim, lim),
    name     = "TWDeConv \u2212\nDecennial",
    labels   = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Residual: TWDeConv 2020 minus Decennial 2020 NH Black share",
    subtitle = "Blue = underestimate; Red = overestimate"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 9)
  )
